name: Valley 1St Community
description_short: OnPoint Community Credit Union is Oregon's largest credit union, serving 645,000+ members across 28 Oregon and 4 Washington counties with nearly 60 branches and comprehensive financial services.
description_long:
OnPoint Community Credit Union was founded in 1932 and has grown to become the largest credit union headquartered in Oregon. With over 645,000 members and $9.6 billion in assets, the institution serves a multi-state membership across 28 Oregon counties (Benton, Clackamas, Clatsop, Columbia, Coos, Crook, Curry, Deschutes, Douglas, Gilliam, Hood River, Jackson, Jefferson, Josephine, Klamath, Lane, Lincoln, Linn, Marion, Morrow, Multnomah, Polk, Sherman, Tillamook, Wasco, Washington, Wheeler, and Yamhill) and 4 Washington counties (Cowlitz, Clark, Lewis, and Skamania). The credit union maintains federal insurance through the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA).

OnPoint offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including member account support, new membership enrollment, personal and commercial loans, home and equity lending, financial planning, ATM services, and notarization. Members also have access to free one-on-one financial counseling, debt management support, and educational resources through a partnership with GreenPath Financial Wellness. The institution operates nearly 60 branches and provides access to 70,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide, with recent expansion including placement of branches inside Fred Meyer locations.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its aggressive branch expansion strategy, having opened the largest branch expansion in credit union history in 2021 when it opened 20 branches within Fred Meyer locations in a single year. As of 2026, OnPoint has opened three additional branches across Oregon and Southwest Washington since January, demonstrating continued commitment to in-person banking access. The institution also maintains strong community engagement, with leadership like branch manager Adriel Pickar serving on local foundation boards and the credit union making charitable donations to support community initiatives.

OnPoint is best suited for individuals and businesses in its service territory who value credit union membership benefits, community reinvestment, and broad branch access. The primary limitation is geographic—membership is restricted to residents or workers in the specified Oregon and Washington counties, making it inaccessible to those outside these regions. Additionally, as a credit union rather than a traditional bank, some members may find fewer online-only product options compared to larger national banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Full member account support and account management
Mobile banking app with check deposit via camera
pros: Largest credit union headquartered in Oregon with $9.6 billion in assets and 645,000+ members
Access to 70,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide despite regional headquarters
Nearly 60 physical branches with aggressive expansion strategy (3 new locations opened in early 2026)
Partnership with GreenPath Financial Wellness for free financial counseling and debt management support
Comprehensive services including personal loans, commercial loans, home/equity lending, and financial planning in one institution
Community investment demonstrated through charitable donations and board participation from leadership
Federal NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
cons: Geographic restriction: membership limited to 28 Oregon counties and 4 Washington counties only
Smaller product ecosystem compared to national mega-banks (no mention of investment products beyond Raymond James affiliate referrals)
Website content does not specify APR ranges, loan terms, or competitive rate information for products offered
best_for: Oregon and Southwest Washington residents seeking community-focused credit union membership with extensive branch access
Small business owners in the service territory needing commercial lending and business account solutions
Consumers seeking free financial counseling and debt management support alongside traditional banking
Members who value surcharge-free ATM access and prefer credit union governance over for-profit bank structures
diagnosis: OnPoint is best for residents and workers in its 32-county service area (28 Oregon + 4 Washington counties) who want full-service banking from a large, community-focused credit union with extensive branch and ATM access. The critical caveat is geographic eligibility—those outside the specified counties cannot join regardless of product appeal.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
